channel.title: NPR Topics: Politics
channel.description: NPR's expanded coverage of U.S. and world politics, the latest news from Congress and the White House and elections.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump raked in more than $1B through crypto ventures, federal filing shows
item.description: President Trump and his family took in more than $1 billion last year through crypto businesses, a federal filing released Wednesday revealed.
item.content:encoded: President Trump and his family took in more than $1 billion last year through crypto businesses, a federal filing released Wednesday revealed.
item.dc:creator: Linda Kenyon

item.title: Former ethics lawyer says Trump's crypto poses 'clear conflict of interest'
item.description: Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."
item.content:encoded:
Former White House ethics lawyer Richard Painter says President Trump "stands alone" in having substantial financial conflicts of interest and that, "for every other executive branch official, it would be a violation."

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Democratic socialists pose a challenge for the party as midterms approach
item.description: A string of high-profile victories by democratic socialists is posing a challenge for Democrats as they look ahead to midterms and seek a path back to the majority in Congress.
item.content:encoded: A string of high-profile victories by democratic socialists is posing a challenge for Democrats as they look ahead to midterms and seek a path back to the majority in Congress.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: The rise of democratic socialists and what it means for the party
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Brad Lander, Democratic nominee for New York's 10th Congressional District, about the rise of democratic socialists in the Democratic Party.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with Brad Lander, Democratic nominee for New York's 10th Congressional District, about the rise of democratic socialists in the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Trump takes first flight on new Air Force One gifted by the Qatari government
item.description: President Trump took his first flight on the new Air Force One yesterday. The Boeing 747 luxury jet was given to the U.S. by the government of Qatar, raising concerns about ethics and security.
item.content:encoded: President Trump took his first flight on the new Air Force One yesterday. The Boeing 747 luxury jet was given to the U.S. by the government of Qatar, raising concerns about ethics and security.
item.dc:creator: Joel Rose

item.title: How was the new Air Force One prepared for flight?
item.description: Has President Trump's new Qatari plane truly been put through all the security and maintenance checks required of Air Force One?
item.content:encoded: Has President Trump's new Qatari plane truly been put through all the security and maintenance checks required of Air Force One?
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Democratic socialists are on a winning streak. Here's what that means for Democrats
item.description: Last week, a pair of democratic socialists won competitive primary matchups in New York. Another in Philadelphia back in May, and on Tuesday in Denver, it happened again.
item.content:encoded: Last week, a pair of democratic socialists won competitive primary matchups in New York. Another in Philadelphia back in May, and on Tuesday in Denver, it happened again.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Trump's crypto earnings far outpace the businesses he spent decades building
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Bernard Condon of the AP about how President Trump and his family raked in more than a billion dollars last year through crypto ventures.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow talks with Bernard Condon of the AP about how President Trump and his family raked in more than a billion dollars last year through crypto ventures.
item.dc:creator: Michelle Aslam

item.title: SCOTUS upholds birthright citizenship. And, key results from Colorado's primaries
item.description: The Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship on constitutional grounds on the last day of its term. And, a Democratic socialist has won the Democratic primary for a U.S. House seat in Denver.
item.content:encoded:
The Supreme Court upheld birthright citizenship on constitutional grounds on the last day of its term. And, a Democratic socialist has won the Democratic primary for a U.S. House seat in Denver.

(Image credit: Tom Brenner)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: A federal law bans late voter roll purges. Republicans are pushing to reinterpret it
item.description: There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.
item.content:encoded:
There's a ban on most states systematically purging voter rolls within 90 days before Election Day. Republicans are pushing courts to reinterpret that longstanding protection for eligible voters.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang
